entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A C4 crop cultivated for grain and stover in rain-fed farming systems of tropics and subtropics.

entities.name: TROPICS AND SUBTROPICS
entities.description: Regions where pearl millet is cultivated.

entities.name: INDIAN SUBCONTINENT
entities.description: A region where pearl millet is cultivated.

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: A region where pearl millet is cultivated.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A water stress condition that affects crop growth and yield.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: A global phenomenon predicted to exacerbate the intensity of drought.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain growth and yield under drought stress.

entities.name: TARGET POPULATIONS OF ENVIRONMENTS (TPES)
entities.description: Environments where pearl millet is grown, characterized by variable drought timing, intensity, and duration.

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING
entities.description: Platforms used to screen large populations or germplasm for drought-adaptive traits.

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic resources including wild and cultivated accessions used in breeding.

entities.name: DROUGHT-ADAPTIVE TRAITS
entities.description: Traits that allow a plant to adapt to drought-stress environments.

entities.name: HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: The process of crossing adapted landraces with selected elite genetic material.

entities.name: LANDRACES
entities.description: Locally adapted traditional varieties of a crop.

entities.name: ELITE GENETIC MATERIAL
entities.description: High-performing genetic material used in breeding programs.

entities.name: GENOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Tools and data used to explore genetic diversity and mapping.

entities.name: LINKAGE MAPPING
entities.description: A method used to identify Quantitative Trait Loci (QTLs).

entities.name: QTLS
entities.description: Quantitative Trait Loci associated with specific traits.

entities.name: MARKER-TRAIT ASSOCIATION
entities.description: A method used to associate genetic markers with specific traits.

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method using genome-wide markers to predict trait performance.

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT GENOTYPING
entities.description: Low-cost platforms for rapid genotyping of large populations.

entities.name: MARKERS ASSISTED SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding approach using markers to select for desired traits.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: Traditional breeding programs targeting specific traits like drought tolerance.

entities.name: NEXT-GENERATION SEQUENCING
entities.description: Technology used to sequence genomes and identify candidate genes and markers.

entities.name: MICRO-ENVIRONMENTAL MODELING
entities.description: A method used to understand the interaction between the plant and its environment.

entities.name: CANDIDATE GENES
entities.description: Genes suspected to be involved in a particular trait.

entities.name: MARKERS
entities.description: DNA sequences used to identify specific locations on a chromosome.

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING
entities.description: The application of molecular biology and genomics to crop improvement.

entities.name: MODERN GENOMICS
entities.description: The use of genomic tools to enhance crop development.

entities.name: MOLECULAR BIOLOGY
entities.description: The study of biological activity at the molecular level used in breeding.

entities.name: SHUTTLE BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding strategy to accelerate the development of cultivars.
